steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: When a value increases by 148%, the new amount is 100% + 148% = 248% of the original, or $$1 + 1.48 = 2.48$$ times the original. When a value decreases by 14%, it becomes 100% - 14% = 86% of what it was, or $$1 - 0.14 = 0.86$$ times that amount.

steps.title: Apply both changes sequentially
steps.content: The value is first multiplied by 2.48 (the 148% increase from 2014 to 2015), then multiplied by 0.86 (the 14% decrease from 2015 to 2016). The combined effect multiplies the original value by $$2.48 \times 0.86$$.

steps.title: Calculate the net multiplier and convert to percentage
steps.content: Computing the product: $$2.48 \times 0.86 = 2.1328$$ This means the value at the end of 2016 is 2.1328 times its value at the end of 2014. A multiplier of 2.1328 corresponds to an increase of $$2.1328 - 1 = 1.1328 = 113.28\%$$. Therefore, the correct answer is choice A.
